shadows.name: comparison-drawer
shadows.purpose: Separates the fixed artifact comparison surface from the catalog.

motion.name: constellation-draw
motion.purpose: Draws model lineage connectors once; disabled for reduced motion.

components.name: Selected Control
components.css: .ds-selected { background: var(--foreground); color: var(--background); border: 1px solid var(--foreground); border-radius: 7px; padding: 6px 12px; font: 600 13px var(--font-plex-sans), Arial, sans-serif; } .ds-selected:hover { opacity: 0.92; } .ds-selected:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--meta); outline-offset: 1px; }

components.name: Outline Control
components.description: Unselected segment, release, size, or variant.
components.css: .ds-outline { background: var(--panel); color: var(--foreground); border: 1px solid var(--line); border-radius: 7px; padding: 6px 12px; font: 600 13px var(--font-plex-sans), Arial, sans-serif; } .ds-outline:hover { border-color: var(--foreground); } .ds-outline:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--meta); outline-offset: 1px; }

components.name: VRAM Filter Pill
components.description: Generic capacity selection, never a recommendation.
components.css: .ds-filter { background: var(--foreground); color: var(--background); border: 1px solid var(--foreground); border-radius: 9999px; padding: 4px 12px; font: 600 12.5px var(--font-plex-sans), Arial, sans-serif; } .ds-filter:hover { opacity: 0.92; } .ds-filter:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--meta); outline-offset: 1px; }

components.name: Search Field
components.description: Filters the active catalog or model comparison view.
components.html: <input class="ds-search" type="search" placeholder="Search models, families, vendors…">
components.css: .ds-search { width: 320px; background: var(--panel); color: var(--foreground); border: 1px solid var(--line); border-radius: 7px; padding: 6px 10px; font: 400 13.5px var(--font-plex-sans), Arial, sans-serif; } .ds-search:hover { border-color: var(--muted); } .ds-search:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--meta); outline-offset: 1px; }

components.name: Property Chip
components.description: Immutable model property or provenance label.
components.css: .ds-chip { display: inline-flex; background: var(--panel-2); color: var(--muted); border-radius: 5px; padding: 1px 6px; font: 500 11.5px var(--font-plex-mono), monospace; }

components.name: Reading Panel
components.description: Functional model selection, artifacts, or benchmark region.
components.css: .ds-panel { background: var(--panel); color: var(--foreground); border: 1px solid var(--line); border-radius: 10px; padding: 14px 16px; font: 400 13px var(--font-plex-sans), Arial, sans-serif; } .ds-panel strong { font-size: 11px; letter-spacing: 0.12em; }

narrative.overview: Akashic Computer should feel like a carefully maintained reference work that happens to be interactive. The interface is scholarly, calm, and exact. Dense technical information is welcome when its hierarchy remains obvious and every color or badge carries meaning. The system uses a restrained paper-and-ink composition with editorial serif moments, utilitarian sans-serif controls, and monospaced data.
keyCharacteristics: Warm paper surfaces with dark ink and thin structural rules.
Editorial model names paired with compact technical metadata.
Dense tables that remain factual, sortable, and expandable.
Semantic color reserved for verification, caution, metadata, and constraints.
Flat surfaces at rest with no decorative elevation.

rules.name: The Evidence Color Rule
rules.body: Semantic colors only communicate provenance, confidence, metadata, or constraints. They never decorate empty space.

rules.name: The Paper Dominance Rule
rules.body: Ink and paper must carry the composition before any accent color appears.

rules.name: The Editorial Identity Rule
rules.body: Serif type names the product and models. It never appears inside buttons, filters, badges, or utility labels.

rules.name: The Data Alignment Rule
rules.body: Comparable numbers use IBM Plex Mono and tabular numerals.

rules.name: The Flat Catalog Rule
rules.body: Static panels never use shadows. If a panel needs emphasis, strengthen its hierarchy or border instead.

dos: Do preserve the family, release, size, variant, and artifact hierarchy.
Do show the complete catalog before applying optional VRAM filtering.
Do use provenance and confidence labels wherever data origins differ.
Do keep model comparison benchmark-led and artifact comparison explicitly separate.
Do use semantic color only when the same state is also expressed in text or symbols.
Do keep the current warm paper, serif identity, sans utility, and mono data pairing.
donts: Don't make Akashic Computer feel like a marketing site or affiliate buying guide.
Don't introduce a generic SaaS dashboard or ornamental card grid.
Don't use decorative gradients, glassmorphism, colored side stripes, or gradient text.
Don't label one model or artifact as universally best or recommended.
Don't turn a benchmark leaderboard into promotional ranking language.
Don't let hardware-first framing hide the broader model ecosystem.
Don't use display typography in buttons, filters, badges, or data cells.
